other_observers: none; I found the bird, not seen again.
specific_location: Valco Ponds, west of the Valco Ponds parking lot.
description: I was walking east along a dirt road, west of the Valco Ponds parking lot, when a bird flushed out off the ground and heading north thru the trees and out of sight. I probably only saw the bird for 10 seconds, as it flew. The bird had a long bill, buffy orange color underneath, it was plump looking. The back seemed more gray in color, not brown like snipes, the wings were brown, without any stripes. I wasn't expecting this bird at all, though the sounds the bird was making, plus the odd plump shape, and colors, elimnate all other possiblities. I remember looking at the ground where the bird flushed up, and there were dead leaves, below the cottonwoods.Behaviors: Flushed up from the ground, flying north, probably went across the river into Rock Canyon. I never could find the bird again.Call: In flight, making loud twittering sounds with its wings.Plumage: unknown
